name: Baked Ziti
tagline: Ziti baked with marinara, mozzarella, and a ricotta layer
highlight: Crispy edges, creamy middle, no nuts in sight

hero.alt: Baked ziti with bubbling cheese top and tomato pasta beneath
hero.caption: baked ziti — bubbly, browned

healthFacts: Combining ricotta with the pasta increases the protein per serving by ~6g.
Tomato sauce + olive oil = better lycopene absorption than raw tomato.

steps.title: Cook ziti underdone
steps.detail: Boil ziti in heavily salted water 2 minutes less than package time — it'll finish in the oven. Drain.
steps.tip: Underdone pasta absorbs sauce instead of going mushy in the oven.

steps.title: Mix the ricotta
steps.detail: Stir ricotta, egg, half the parmesan, salt, and pepper. This becomes the creamy middle layer.
steps.tip: Egg in ricotta makes it set instead of running everywhere.

steps.title: Layer the dish
steps.detail: Toss pasta with most of the marinara. Layer half in a baking dish; dollop ricotta and half the mozzarella; top with remaining pasta, sauce, mozzarella, and parmesan.
steps.tip: Don't try to spread the ricotta — dollops melt into pockets, which is exactly what you want.

steps.title: Bake until bubbly
steps.detail: Bake covered at 375°F for 20 minutes, then uncovered 10 minutes more for golden top. Rest 5 minutes; tear basil over.
steps.tip: Rest matters — straight from the oven, the layers slide.
